READING THE LEAFLET
LEARNING THE BACKGROUND
Context refers to the social, cultural, relational, and historical factors that surround communication.
Understanding the context of interactions is crucial for effective dialogue and engagement.

GENERATING MEANING
Meaning is constructed through a combination of language, individual perceptions, and social interactions.
Collaborative processes enhance the generation of meaning in group settings.

MAKING SENSE OF LOCAL KNOWLEDGE
Local knowledge includes community traditions, practices, and experiences that shape understanding.
Recognizing and validating local knowledge fosters inclusion and respect in communication.

INTERNALIZING THE PROBLEM
Addressing problems involves reflecting on personal biases, understanding community dynamics, and recognizing areas for improvement.
Engagement with the community can lead to better solutions and awareness of underlying issues.

UNDERSTANDING THE FORMATION OF SOCIETY
Society is shaped by historical events, social structures, and cultural influences.
Understanding these elements is essential for effective social change and communication strategies.

LEARNING THE DRUM AND PERFORMING
   As we study the drums, we want to connect to the emotional world of ourselves as musicians and performers. We each develop our own unique sound, feel and groove while continuing to check in with other drummers, teachers and percussionists to grow and expand our skills.  
   Whether you aim to join a marching band, play in a jazz combo, or put together a rock band, your sound is your own and can be contributed to your group in a healthy and productive way. Embodying the spirit of the drum is as much about your character as it is about the now. Keep your mind open and challenge yourself to step outside the box from time to time. You may discover new areas of interest as a percussionist and musician.

EQUAL CREDIT OPPORTUNITY ACT NOTICE – FAIRWAY INDEPENDENT MORTGAGE Equal Credit Opportunity Act Notice and Fair Lending Disclosure LOAN 1492545683 | FAIRWAY INDEPENDENT MORTGAGE | 02/06/2024 This Equal Credit Opportunity Act Notice is furnished to Dustin Morgan by Fairway Independent Mortgage in connection with loan number 1492545683 and the credit transaction concerning the property located at 1160 Anderson Loaf Suite 816, Ashleyworth, UT 38931. The federal Equal Credit Opportunity Act and its implementing regulation, Regulation B, prohibit creditors from discriminating against credit applicants on certain prohibited bases and confer upon you important rights with respect to the evaluation of your application, the disposition of your request for credit, and your access to the reason underlying any action taken. Fairway Independent Mortgage is committed to extending credit to all creditworthy applicants in a fair, consistent, and lawful manner, and this disclosure summarizes the protections, obligations, and remedies that govern the credit transaction described above as of February 06, 2024. 1. PROHIBITED BASES OF DISCRIMINATION The Equal Credit Opportunity Act and Regulation B make it unlawful for any creditor, including Fairway Independent Mortgage, to discriminate against an applicant with respect to any aspect of a credit transaction on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, age, or because you receive income from a public assistance program or have exercised any right under the Consumer Credit Protection Act. The law also prohibits a creditor from discriminating against an applicant because of all or part of the applicant's income derives from a public assistance program. Fairway Independent Mortgage is committed to avoiding discrimination and encourages applicants to apply regardless of their background. Fairway Independent Mortgage will not base any credit decision on these factors. Lenders are also prohibited from discouraging an applicant from applying for credit on the basis of these characteristics. Additionally, Fairway Independent Mortgage evaluates income from all sources to assess your creditworthiness. If you feel you have been discriminated against, please contact Fairway Independent Mortgage immediately. If you believe that you have been discriminated against on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, age, or because you receive income from a public assistance program or because you have exercised any right under the Consumer Credit Protection Act, you may file a complaint with the Federal Trade Commission, Bureau of Consumer Protection. At the same time, Fairway Independent Mortgage evaluates income from all sources to assess your creditworthiness. If you feel you have been discriminated against, please contact Fairway Independent Mortgage immediately. If you believe that you have been discriminated against on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, age, or because you receive income from a public assistance program or because you have exercised any right under the Consumer Credit Protection Act, you may file a complaint with the Federal Trade Commission, Bureau of Consumer Protection.

designed to ensure that personnel involved in the origination, underwriting, and servicing of credit adhere to these requirements at every stage of the transaction associated with loan number 1462546853.

1. *TREATMENT OF MARITAL STATUS*  
Consistent with Regulation B, Fairway Independent Mortgage will not refuse to extend credit, nor will it terminate unfairly after the loan’s extension of credit, solely on the basis of the marital status of Dustin Morgan, except where such consideration is expressly permitted or required by applicable law. You may apply for credit in your own name, and a creditor may not require the signature of a spouse or other person, other than a joint applicant, on any credit instrument if you qualify under the creditor’s standards of credworthiness for the amount and terms of the credit requested.

Where the value of jointly owned property, or property located in a community property jurisdiction, is relied upon to support a creditworthiness decision, additional signatures may be required to the extent required to satisfy state or federal statutes, and shall be done consistent with the law of the applicable UT jurisdiction. Any such requirement will be applied without regard to marital status and only to the extent reasonably necessary to protect the creditor’s legitimate interest in the collateral securing loan number 1462546853.

If you are married, you are not required to have credit history that reflects accounts and your spouse under the other of you both being reported in the name of each spouse. Upon request, Fairway Independent Mortgage will furnish information to consumer reporting agencies in that manner, including Dustin Morgan to explain an independent credit history, subject to the accuracy of the underlying account records and the requirements of applicable law.

3. *NOTIFICATION OF ACTION TAKEN*  
Regulation B requires Fairway Independent Mortgage to notify Dustin Morgan of the action taken on an application for credit within the time periods prescribed by law, generally within thirty (30) days following a completed application or concerning the credit requested. That action is, in general, a counteroffer, a denial and subject to conditions, an incomplete application, or a denial. Where a counteroffer is made and you opt to expressly accept our offer, Fairway Independent Mortgage may then proceed with the process and notice roles outlined under Regulation B.

In applications regarding matters that Dustin Morgan can complete, Fairway Independent Mortgage intends, if providing a decision in written form, to identify you by your name and other information as is necessary for identification to ensure we are providing you with the appropriate reasonable information in relation to the application. The period afforded for completing information will be made with further accordance.

A notice of action taken will be provided in a written form, and it will identify Fairway Independent Mortgage as the entity acting under federal or UT consumer-protection statutes applicable to the credit transaction described above.

1. RIGHT TO A STATEMENT OF REASONS When Fairway Independent Mortgage takes adverse action on an application for credit, or on an existing account, you have the right under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act to a statement of the specific reasons for that action. Adverse action includes a denial or revocation of credit, a change in terms of an existing account that is not favorable to Dustin Morgan and that is not applied to all or substantially all of a class of the creditor's accounts, and a refusal to grant credit in substantially the amount or on substantially the terms requested in an application. The statement of specific reasons will either be provided automatically with the notice of adverse action or be made available upon your timely request. If the reasons are not disclosed in the initial notice, Fairway Independent Mortgage will include a statement of your right to request the reasons within sixty (60) days of the notification, together with the name, address, and telephone number of the person or office from which the reasons may be obtained. Upon a timely oral or written request, Fairway Independent Mortgage will furnish the specific reasons in writing within a reasonable period of time, generally not exceeding thirty (30) days. The reasons disclosed will be specific and will indicate the principal factors that influenced the action taken, irrespective of whether the action was based on internal standards or policies, or that the applicant failed to achieve a qualifying score, or that other reasons were insufficient. Any instruments of credit decisioning with loan number 1462548633 will accurately reflect the actual basis upon which the credit decision was made. 5. ADVERSE ACTION AND CREDIT SCORING If Fairway Independent Mortgage bases its credit decision in whole or in part on information obtained from a consumer reporting agency, the notice of adverse action provided to Dustin Morgan will identify the name of the consumer reporting agency, the address, and telephone number of such agency that furnished the notice, and the reason for the adverse action taken with respect to the credit. The notice will further advise you of your right to obtain a free copy of your consumer report from the reporting agency, and you may wish to contact them directly to obtain it. If Fairway Independent Mortgage takes adverse action based in whole or in part on information derived from a consumer reporting agency, Fairway Independent Mortgage will disclose the score used, the range of possible scores, and a key indicating the effect of the score on the terms that were provided by Fairway Independent Mortgage to the entity that provided the information to the extent required by applicable law. These disclosures are made in accordance with the Fair Housing Act. The notice will include the following statement: "This notice is based on information derived from consumer reporting agencies; your credit score can change over time." Fairway Independent Mortgage will retain such documentation as required under the Fair Credit Reporting Act and Regulation B.

6. GOVERNMENT MONITORING INFORMATION

For applications to purchase or refinance a dwelling occupied or to be occupied as a principal residence, federal law requires Fairway Independent Mortgage to request certain information regarding the applicant's ethnicity, race, and sex for the purpose of monitoring compliance with the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, the Home Mortgage Disclosure Act, and other fair lending statutes. The furnishing of this information by Dustin Morgan is voluntary, and Fairway Independent Mortgage may not discriminate on the basis of this information, or on the basis of whether you choose to provide it, in any aspect of the credit transaction.

If you choose not to furnish the requested monitoring information, federal regulations may require Fairway Independent Mortgage to note the information on the basis of visual observation, surname, or both, for an application taken in person. This collection is undertaken solely for governmental monitoring purposes and statistical purposes and plays no part in the evaluation of the creditworthiness of Dustin Morgan or the decision to extend, deny, or condition the credit associated with loan number 142564863.

Monitoring information collected by Fairway Independent Mortgage is maintained, reported, and used in accordance with applicable federal and UT law and is segregated from the factors used in underwriting the transaction. Fairway Independent Mortgage will not use this information for any purpose inconsistent with the regulatory objectives for which it is gathered.

7. APPRAISALS AND VALUATIONS

In connection with an application for credit to be secured by a first lien on a dwelling, Fairway Independent Mortgage will provide Dustin Morgan with copies of all appraisals and other written valuations developed in connection with the application, except as limited by applicable regulations. This information will be furnished promptly upon request and in accordance with Regulation B and will not charge for the copies of the appraisal or valuation.

The appraisal report will be sent to the property identified as 1160 Anderson Loaf Suite 818, Ahemyston, UT 38831 and is reasonable necessary to assess the creditworthiness of Dustin Morgan and the sufficiency of any collateral. Fairway Independent Mortgage will not inquire about Dustin Morgan and the sufficiency of any collateral.

8. INFORMATION REQUESTS AND OPTIONAL DISCLOSURES

In evaluating your application, Fairway Independent Mortgage may request only the information that is permissible under Regulation B. And that is reasonably necessary to assess the creditworthiness of Dustin Morgan and the sufficiency of any collateral. Fairway Independent Mortgage will not inquire about Dustin Morgan and the sufficiency of any collateral.

1. ADMINISTERING FEDERAL AGENCY
The federal agency that administers compliance with the Equal Credit Opportunity Act with respect to Fairway Independent Mortgage is identified in the notice of action taken or the adverse action notice provided in connection with the credit transaction. Depending on the situation, enforcement may be pursued in conjunction with the applicable regulatory agency, the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, a federal banking agency, or the Federal Trade Commission by naming and addressing your complaint to:

Dustin Morgan
Fairway Independent Mortgage
at 78 Thomas Hill, L Lake Suamico, WI 09316 or by contacting us at 8882968540. The Federal Credit Opportunity Act Notice is provided for your information regarding the federal agency and its commitment to equitable credit, and does not alter the terms of any engagement you have with Dustin Morgan.

2. RECORD RETENTION AND RESERVATION OF RIGHTS
Fairway Independent Mortgage retains applications, notices, and supporting documentation relating to the credit transaction identified above for the periods mandated by Regulation B and other applicable law. This record retention policy covers types of records such as applications, notices, examinations, or enforcement proceedings undertaken by the administering federal agency. The intent of these records is intended to facilitate compliance monitoring and to enable Fairway Independent

Mortgage to substantiate, where necessary, the basis upon which credit decisions concerning Dustin Morgan were made. 

Nothing contained in this notice waives, limits or diminishes any right, protection, or remedy available to Dustin Morgan under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, the Fair Credit Reporting Act, the Fair Housing Act, or any other applicable federal or state consumer protection statute. The extent any provision of this disclosure is found to conflict with controlling law, the governing statute or regulation shall prevail, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

This notice is current as of February 06, 2024 and reflects the fair-lending obligations applicable to Fairway Independent Mortgage as of that date. Fairway Independent Mortgage reserves the right to update this disclosure to conform to changes in applicable law or regulatory guidance occurring during 2024 or thereafter, and any such revision will be applied prospectively in accordance with governing authority.
